title: Black Monday: US Markets Halt Trading for First Time in Years
summary: Circuit breakers tripped as the S&P 500 plunged 7%. A global pandemic mixed with an oil price war sparks unprecedented terror in global markets. Apple (AAPL) stores closed globally, and bottling operations for Coke (KO) face supply chain collapse.

title: Worst Day Since 1987: Dow Crashes Another 10%
summary: Traders call it the worst panic since Black Monday of 1987. The VIX volatility index skyrockets to historic highs. Widespread lockdowns and travel bans are destroying value across all sectors.

title: Panic Deepens: Fed's Emergency Rate Cut Fails to Calm Markets
summary: Despite the Federal Reserve slashing interest rates to zero, the market triggered another Level 1 circuit breaker immediately at the open. The global economy is grinding to a complete halt.

title: Unemployment Claims Likely to Shatter Records
summary: As states force non-essential businesses to close, economists project unemployment numbers not seen since the Great Depression. Investor sentiment shows zero signs of a bottom.
